name: Glen Canyon Park
note: Archive documents linked to this place; source labels honour the SFPL-vs-Democracy's-Library distinction.

documents.title: Agendas and Minutes / Visual Arts Committee
documents.creator: Arts Commission of San Francisco. Visual Arts Committee
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: Arts Commission Visual Arts Committee minutes selecting three finalists for the Glen Canyon Park Recreation Center public art project.

documents.title: 2008 Clean & Safe Neighborhood Parks bond
documents.creator: San Francisco (Calif.)
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: The 2008 parks bond's project page for Glen Canyon Park — a 66.6-acre park with a 1934 recreation center and 1960 Silver Tree Day Camp — scoping renovation of the rec center, play area, day camp, courts and field.

documents.title: Diamond Heights : a report on the tentative redevelopment plan
documents.creator: San Francisco Redevelopment Agency (San Francisco, Calif.)
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1951 Diamond Heights redevelopment report describing the canyon's day camp, rock outcroppings and creek adjoining the planned neighborhood.

documents.title: Two commercial sites, Diamond Heights, San Francisco
documents.creator: San Francisco Redevelopment Agency (San Francisco, Calif.)
documents.source_label: Internet Archive — San Francisco Public Library partnership scans
documents.gloss: A 1962 Redevelopment Agency prospectus describing Glen Canyon Park as an 80-acre naturally wooded canyon open for recreation, with a park building already completed.
